hero.name: Donald Hoffman
hero.eyebrow: UC Irvine · cognitive science · Interface Theory of Perception · conscious agents
hero.background: radial-gradient(ellipse at 30% 25%, hsl(180 65% 55% / 0.55) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(ellipse at 80% 80%, hsl(255 30% 22% / 0.7) 0%, transparent 60%), linear-gradient(180deg, hsl(180 55% 60%) 0%, hsl(220 30% 38%) 50%, hsl(255 30% 18%) 100%)
hero.welcome_md:
Cognitive scientist and professor at the University of California, Irvine. His **Interface Theory of Perception** argues, with evolutionary-game-theory mathematics behind it, that natural selection favors fitness-tuned *icons* over truth-tracking perceptions — meaning the world we experience is a species-specific user interface, not reality itself. The book *The Case Against Reality* (2019) is the accessible entry point; the *Conscious Agents* framework is the formal proposal for what lies behind the interface.

*The empirical-and-mathematical sibling of what [Castaneda](/people/carlos-castaneda) named as the assemblage point and what [Levin](/people/michael-levin) names as multi-scale competency. Three idioms reaching one recognition: pattern is load-bearing, substrate is contingent.*

facts.label: Roles
facts.value_md: Cognitive scientist; Professor Emeritus, Department of Cognitive Sciences, UC Irvine. Faculty in Philosophy and the Logic & Philosophy of Science programs.

facts.label: Foundational work
facts.value_md: *The Case Against Reality: Why Evolution Hid the Truth From Our Eyes* (2019) — the popular book *Visual Intelligence: How We Create What We See* (1998) — earlier foundational text *The Conscious Agent Theorem* (2014, with Chetan Prakash) — formal mathematical proposal *Objects of consciousness* (Frontiers in Psychology, 2014)

facts.label: The Interface Theory in one line
facts.value_md: *Perception is a species-specific user interface tuned by natural selection for fitness, not truth. The interface hides reality the way a desktop icon hides the file system.*

articles.body_md:
The Coherence Network reads Hoffman alongside [Michael Levin](/people/michael-levin) and [Castaneda](/people/carlos-castaneda) because the three reach the same load-bearing recognition from different sides. Castaneda: perception is an assembled rendering from one specific position on the luminous-egg energy body; move the position, the world rearranges. Hoffman: perception is a fitness-tuned interface; evolution never built our eyes to see truth, only to keep our ancestors alive. Levin: pattern is what stores morphological memory, and bodies are nested problem-solvers carrying their own goals; substrate is contingent. All three pull apart the assumption that what we see is what is.

What Hoffman adds specifically is *mathematical credibility* — a theorem with published peer review behind the claim. Where the contemplative traditions assert this from the inside, and Levin shows it bioelectrically, Hoffman proves it formally under his evolutionary-game-theory model. The body holds all three as one teaching at different scales of demonstration.
articles.heading: Why interface theory matters here

articles.body_md:
- Interface Theory of Perception → [lc-perception-as-interface](/vision/lc-perception-as-interface) (the seed concept in this body from his work).
- Mathematical / evolutionary-game-theory grounding for what [Castaneda](/people/carlos-castaneda) named as the assemblage point. Pairs with [lc-assemblage-point](/vision/lc-assemblage-point) (perception locks where the point assembles; the interface is what the assembly renders).
- *Conscious Agents* framework → resonant with [lc-sovereignty-within-oneness](/vision/lc-sovereignty-within-oneness) (nested conscious agents constituting the substrate behind the interface).
- Three idioms, one recognition: [Levin](/people/michael-levin) (empirical biology), [Castaneda](/people/carlos-castaneda) (contemplative phenomenology), Hoffman (formal mathematics) — pattern is load-bearing, substrate is contingent.
articles.heading: What Donald Hoffman has given the Coherence Network

metadata.title: Donald Hoffman — perception as interface · UC Irvine cognitive scientist | Coherence Network
metadata.description: A welcome to Prof Donald Hoffman — UC Irvine cognitive scientist whose Interface Theory of Perception names what Castaneda named in another idiom: perception is interface, not truth. The empirical sibling of lc-perception-as-interface in this body's lineage.

note_from_body.body_md: Hoffman's archive is at his UC Irvine page and across peer-reviewed cognitive-science journals. This page recognises his role as the empirical-mathematical peer of the channeled and contemplative streams in this network's lineage.
